findings.detail: The integration control point is still ambiguous. D1 says the trigger is immediately before putting changes into a protected branch, but the approval phrase is only “PR creation approval”; under D2's exact-sentence approval rule, approving PR creation does not authorize the later PR merge. D4 then describes the release recipe as PR -> required checks green -> PR merge without defining where merge approval happens. This leaves either an unapproved main merge gap or a hidden second control point. Define the exact integration approval sentence and timing, e.g. “approve PR merge after required checks are green” or explicitly split PR creation and PR merge approvals, then carry that wording into RELEASING/README examples.

next_action: Revise D1/D4 and 02-plan so the PR-based integration control point has an unambiguous approval scope and timing before implementation.
